evidence_text: DUBAI, June 7 - Port authorities in Oman and the United Arab Emirates said they will extend a joint customs digitization pilot to speed cargo documentation for feeder and coastal vessels, according to statements issued on Sunday. Shipping agents said the changes affect port-side processing rather than routing decisions through the Strait of Hormuz.

rationale: This is topically related because it involves Gulf ports and shipping operations, but it does not bear on whether IMF PortWatch will record enough Strait of Hormuz arrivals to reach the 60 threshold.
